item.title: U.S. strikes 2 Iranian ports as American warships come under fire
item.description: Three U.S. Navy destroyers transiting the Strait of Hormuz came under attack on Thursday, and the U.S. struck on two Iranian ports abutting the strait, putting into question an increasingly fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire.

item.title: Tennessee approves new congressional map dissolving majority Black district
item.description: Tennessee Republicans earlier Thursday approved a measure to overturn the state's ban on mid-decade redistricting.

item.title: AAPI Americans more likely than others to say recent Trump policies negatively impacted them: study
item.description: According to a new study from the Asian American Foundation, Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders are more likely than other Americans to say that recent federal policy changes have negatively impacted their communities. Norman Chen, its CEO, joins "The Daily Report" with more.

item.title: Are the Trump administration's efforts to lower drug costs working?
item.description: President Trump has made cutting drug prices a centerpiece of his second term. He's announced deals with major drug companies and launched TrumpRx, where cash-paying patients could find discounted medicines. But a new analysis finds that the reality is more complicated. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.

item.title: L.A. mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt taps into angst about homelessness, wildfire response
item.description: The race for Los Angeles mayor is heating up following a spirited first debate on Wednesday night. Spencer Pratt, a registered Republican, spoke with CBS News correspondent Adam Yamaguchi about his campaign in the non-partisan race.

item.title: Trump calls Iran's attack in Strait of Hormuz a "love tap"
item.description: The U.S. confirmed that three of its military vessels came under Iranian fire in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday. According to CENTCOM, no U.S. assets were struck and forces were able to intercept the attack. CBS News national security contributor Sam Vinograd has more.

item.title: Man who was mistakenly interviewed on TV shares lesson in staying calm
item.description: Guy Goma walked into a BBC interview for an IT job, but was mistaken for an expert and was rushed on air. Twenty years later, he reflects on how a screw-up can turn into a moment of grace.

item.title: Spencer Pratt compares his fledgling political career to Obama's national rise
item.description: Spencer Pratt has said he was compelled to launch his unlikely mayoral run after his home was destroyed in last year's devastating Los Angeles wildfires.

item.title: 5 states monitoring passengers who departed ship stricken by hantavirus
item.description: American passengers who left the MV Hondius cruise ship in April are being monitored for hantavirus in at least five states, health officials said.

item.title: U.S. launches "self-defense strikes" on Iran, says warships came under fire
item.description: As President Trump again voiced optimism that Iran will "make a deal" to end the war, Tehran declared itself the regulator of Strait of Hormuz shipping.

item.title: 14 tornadoes reported in Mississippi as storms ravage homes, knock out power
item.description: The storms collapsed hundreds of homes, downed trees and knocked out power lines in multiple counties, officials said.

item.title: Texas courtroom translator released from ICE speaks out: "Constantly watching over my shoulder"
item.description: After six weeks in custody, a Texas courtroom translator who had worked legally in the U.S. for decades was released. Meenu Batra spoke exclusively with Shanelle Kaul.

item.title: Mother of missing woman in Bahamas says husband has not contacted her
item.description: The search for Michigan mother Lynette Hooker headed into a fifth week as Coast Guard investigators seek to question the owners of a sailboat that was moored near the Hooker's vessel when she went missing. Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: Protests in Tennessee after legislature breaks up majority-Black district
item.description: Tennessee became the latest state to redraw its congressional map, breaking up a majority-Black district and adding to a national controversy over mid-decade redistricting. The move came after last week's Supreme Court ruling, which limited lawmakers' ability to take race into account when drawing districts. Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: Mississippi tornadoes lift roofs, collapse walls, leave many without homes
item.description: Mississippi experienced one of the worst tornado outbreaks of the season on Wednesday. At least 17 people were hurt with widespread damage across several counties. Jason Allen reports from the hard-hit town of Purvis, Mississippi.

item.title: U.S. destroyers in Strait of Hormuz fight off Iranian attacks
item.description: Three U.S. destroyers in the Strait of Hormuz fought off a swarm of Iranian fast-attack boats, the U.S. military said. U.S. forces also hit back, striking launch sites for Iranian missiles and drones, according to U.S. Central Command. Nancy Cordes reports.

item.title: 5 states monitoring people who were on hantavirus cruise ship
item.description: After almost a full day of sailing, the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship is slowly closing in on its destination, about 600 nautical miles away from Spain's Canary Islands. Tom Hanson has more details, and Dr. Jon LaPook has more about the virus.

item.title: Homan: "Discussions going on" when asked about giving status to some immigrants in U.S. illegally
item.description: Border czar Tom Homan deferred to President Trump when asked whether he supports giving legal status to millions of immigrants living in the U.S. illegally but who are otherwise law-abiding. "There's discussions going on," Homan told CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez in an exclusive interview. "I'm involved with some and not others, but I'm not going to get ahead of the president on this."

item.title: Federal judge finds DOGE's elimination of humanities grants "unlawful"
item.description: Three of the nation's major scholarly groups challenged the Trump administration's cuts to humanities grants.

item.title: Increasing number of employers seek job candidates with AI skills, survey finds
item.description: As jobs evolve with the use of artificial intelligence, more employers want workers who are familiar with the technology. Eight in 10 hiring managers considered AI skills a priority in 2025, according to Resume Genius research. CBS News MoneyWatch reporter Megan Cerullo has more.

item.title: 5/7: CBS Evening News
item.description: Health officials work to contain hantavirus outbreak; Iran war impacts consumers.

item.title: Democratic donors recruit influencers in midterm push
item.description: The progressive donor network Democracy Alliance is planning to invest tens of millions of dollars in new media and content creators. It's a shift away from traditional media like broadcast television and paid advertisements. Democracy Alliance president Pamela Shifman joins "The Takeout" to explain the shift in strategy.

item.title: Examining the strength of Trump's presidential pardoning power
item.description: CBS News has exclusively learned that Senate and House Democrats are investigating whether President Trump issued pardons and commutations as part of what is described as a "pay-to-play" dynamic. Lawmakers have sent letters to more than a dozen recipients of executive clemency, seeking documents about payment history and communications with lawyers or those close to the president. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson has more.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: 1962 Cuban Missile Crisis
item.description: For 13 days in October of 1962, the world stood on the brink of nuclear war as the U.S. and the Soviet Union feuded over Soviet missiles stored in Cuba, just off the coast of Florida. Caitlin Huey-Burns introduces a special bulletin that interrupted CBS News Radio's programming.

item.title: U.S. trade court rules against Trump's 10% tariffs
item.description: The tariffs were put in place in February, days after the Supreme Court struck down President Trump's previous round of sweeping "Liberation Day" tariffs.

item.title: Tennessee approves congressional map carving up majority-Black district
item.description: Tennessee lawmakers approved a new congressional map on Thursday that carves up a majority-Black district in Memphis. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: 5/7: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Trump administration waiting for Iranian response on peace proposal; Judge releases apparent Jeffrey Epstein suicide note.

item.title: State Department reviewing all Mexican consulates in U.S. as tensions grow
item.description: The State Department is initiating a review of all 53 Mexican consulates in the United States, a U.S. official said, a move that could lead Secretary of State Marco Rubio to consider ordering the closure of some posts.

item.title: AI is as a top cause of layoffs, accounting for 26% of April's job cuts
item.description: New data shows artificial intelligence is the most cited reason for layoffs, even as economists debate whether it is truly displacing workers.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein's possible suicide note released by judge
item.description: A former cellmate has previously claimed that the note was written by Epstein after a failed suicide attempt less than a month before his death.
